questions.explanation: Beethoven composed Piano Sonata No. 14 in C♯ minor "Quasi una fantasia" (1801), famously known as the Moonlight Sonata. Mozart and Haydn were contemporaries but did not compose this work.

questions.q: Johann Sebastian Bach was a master of which compositional technique?
questions.explanation: Bach is renowned for his mastery of fugal counterpoint, exemplified in works like The Art of Fugue and The Well-Tempered Clavier. While he used other forms, fugue is his defining contribution.

questions.q: In what era did Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart compose the majority of his works?
questions.explanation: Mozart (1756-1791) was a central figure of the Classical era. The Classical period emphasizes clarity, balance, and formal structure—all hallmarks of Mozart's style.

questions.q: How many symphonies did Ludwig van Beethoven compose?
questions.explanation: Beethoven composed 9 symphonies, with his Ninth Symphony (featuring the "Ode to Joy" choral finale) being one of the most celebrated works in classical music. This work bridges Classical and Romantic styles.

questions.q: Which of Beethoven's symphonies includes the text "Ode to Joy"?
questions.explanation: The Ninth Symphony (1824) features Friedrich Schiller's poem "Ode to Joy" in its choral finale. This was Beethoven's only symphony to include vocal soloists and a choir.

questions.q: What is the name of Mozart's opera about a nobleman and a chambermaid?
questions.explanation: The Marriage of Figaro (Le nozze di Figaro, 1786) centers on the scheming of the Count and Figaro. Don Giovanni features a seducer and multiple women; The Magic Flute has a fairy-tale plot.

questions.q: Which composer is known for the "Four Seasons" violin concertos?
questions.explanation: Antonio Vivaldi composed The Four Seasons (Le quattro stagioni, circa 1720), a set of four violin concertos depicting spring, summer, autumn, and winter. It remains one of the most performed Baroque works.

questions.q: What musical form is exemplified by Beethoven's "Moonlight Sonata" first movement?
questions.explanation: The Moonlight Sonata's opening is not in strict sonata form but rather a free fantasia-like movement with improvisatory character. Beethoven titled it "Quasi una fantasia" (almost a fantasy).

questions.q: Which Romantic composer wrote the symphonic poem "Les Préludes"?
questions.explanation: Franz Liszt pioneered the symphonic poem form and composed Les Préludes based on Alphonse de Lamartine's work. Berlioz also composed program music but is not the composer of this piece.

questions.q: What is the name of Handel's famous oratorio containing the "Hallelujah" chorus?
questions.explanation: Handel's Messiah (1741) is an English-language oratorio featuring the iconic "Hallelujah" chorus in Part II. It has become a holiday favorite performed during the Christmas season.
